title: Trump Looks for a Silver Bullet to End the Iran War. There May Be None.
summary: The president is trying to ratchet up the economic pressure on Tehran, but Iran’s government is unlikely to make a deal without a big, face-saving compromise.

title: Indiana’s Primary Elections Will Test Trump’s Influence
summary: Also, about 150 people are stuck on a cruise ship with a deadly outbreak. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: Most Trump-Backed Challengers Beat Indiana Incumbents Who Bucked Trump
summary: President Trump endorsed primary opponents against seven Republican lawmakers who opposed his redistricting push. At least five of the Trump-backed candidates have won their races.

title: Ohio Governor’s Race Set Between Vivek Ramaswamy and Amy Acton
summary: In a close race, Vivek Ramaswamy, the Republican nominee, and Dr. Amy Acton, the Democrat, bring distinctive potential liabilities: his wealth and her time leading Ohio’s pandemic response.

title: Vance Campaigns in Iowa as G.O.P. Fears Rise Ahead of Midterms
summary: The vice president acknowledged economic headwinds, including rising energy and fertilizer costs. “We got a little — a little blip in the Middle East,” he said, referring to the war in Iran.

title: To Rename Its Airport After Trump, Palm Beach Had to License His Name
summary: The Trumps won’t profit from airport-branded merchandise sold on site. But the agreement doesn’t prevent them from profiting off such items sold elsewhere.

title: Judge Refers Justice Dept. Lawyer for Possible Discipline, Calling Out ‘Lack of Candor’
summary: Officials withheld a detainee’s overseas arrest warrant from a federal judge. When she ordered his release, they used the same information to attack her publicly.

title: F.D.A. Blocked Publication of Research Finding Covid and Shingles Vaccines Were Safe
summary: The agency’s scientists and data contractors reviewed millions of patient records for studies that were pulled back before release.

title: When ‘The Late Show With Stephen Colbert’ Goes Away, What Do We Lose?
summary: Our attachment to an institution may seem counterintuitive, especially with comedy, a rebellious art form. But with Stephen Colbert’s program, there was a lot at stake.

title: Apple Reaches $250 Million Settlement Over Claims It Misled People on A.I.
summary: Some iPhone owners will be eligible to receive $25 to $95 over claims that the tech giant oversold its artificial intelligence system, Apple Intelligence.

title: Elon Musk Wanted OpenAI to Go Commercial, Greg Brockman Testifies
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president, testified in a trial pitting Mr. Musk against his company that the world’s richest man was eager to change how it operated as a nonprofit.

title: ‘Avatar’ Suit Focuses on Hot Topic in A.I. Age: A Character’s Face
summary: An actress accused the director James Cameron of stealing her likeness for a digitally created, blue-skinned warrior princess.

title: Was It Art? Was It Fashion? Was It Good?
summary: Guests at the Met Gala had different interpretations of the night’s dress code.

title: How Heidi Klum Got Her 2026 Met Gala Statue Look
summary: “I could eat and use my hands quite comfortably,” Klum said of her intricate Roman statue ensemble, which was inspired by a marble sculpture by Raffaele Monti.

title: Modi’s Triumph in West Bengal Elections Puts Him Closer to an Opposition-Free India
summary: With his triumph in West Bengal state elections, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has moved closer to his dream of an opposition-free India.

title: With Mamdani’s Rise, Muslim Democrats Become Power Players
summary: The Muslim Democratic Club was founded to increase political clout among the city’s Muslims. Now some of its founders are the mayor’s top aides.

title: In Attack on Mamdani, Vornado Chief Likens ‘Tax the Rich’ to Hate Speech
summary: Steven Roth, the chief executive of Vornado, criticized Mayor Zohran Mamdani for singling out the billionaire Ken Griffin in his bid to make the wealthy pay more taxes in New York City.

title: N.Y.P.D. Captain Is Transferred After Calling Mamdani ‘Total Nonsense’
summary: The captain, James G. Wilson, was also filmed calling Democrats a “waste of human race.”

title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rip Into Ukrainian Cities
summary: At least 12 people were killed in Zaporizhzhia, one of the highest tolls from a single attack so far this year. The cities of Dnipro and Kramatorsk were also attacked.

title: New Musicals ‘Schmigadoon!’ and ‘Lost Boys’ Lead List of Tony Nominees
summary: The nominated performers include the film stars Daniel Radcliffe, Rose Byrne, John Lithgow and Lesley Manville.

title: Tony Nominations Snubs and Surprises: Lea Michele Left Out, ‘Titaníque’ Shines
summary: Adrien Brody, Jean Smart and other boldface names were left out, while June Squibb, André De Shields and Layton Williams as an iceberg were among the surprises.

title: Cruise Ship Struck by Hantavirus Is to Head to Canary Islands, W.H.O. Says
summary: The Spanish government will receive the vessel. The World Health Organization said human-to-human transmission may have played a role in the outbreak.

title: Hantavirus Outbreaks Are Rare, but They Aren’t Going Away and There’s No Cure
summary: Since the family of rodent-borne infections were identified in the 1950s, they have turned up all over the world.

title: What Happened When Pope Leo XIV Had to Call Customer Service
summary: Spoiler alert: There was no miracle.

title: With Free Vodka, and a Few Protests, Russia Returns to the Venice Biennale
summary: On Tuesday, Russia opened a pavilion at the Venice Biennale for the first since its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Ukraine’s own exhibit is just a stone’s throw.

title: Venice Biennale: In Photos and Video
summary: Scenes from the highly anticipated art event.

title: The Venice Biennale and Its Many Flashpoints: An Explainer
summary: The sudden death of a curator, the surprise participation of Russia and an unconventional U.S. entry are heating up the art world Olympics, which begins previews Tuesday.

title: The U.S. and China Have a Common Foe. Hint: It’s Not the U.S.S.R.
summary: A new level of global disorder will face the American and Chinese presidents at their summit. Like Mao and Nixon, they face a common threat.

title: A Democrat Who Makes Me Listen
summary: An interview with Representative Jake Auchincloss, a Democrat from the charismatic center.

title: U.S. Sues The New York Times, Claiming Discrimination Against a White Man
summary: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission said the paper had engaged in “unlawful employment practices” against the man, who did not get a sought-after promotion.

title: Louise Arbour Named as Canada’s Governor General
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s appointment of Louise Arbour, a former Supreme Court justice, as governor general, sends a message beyond Canada.

title: Democrats Urge N.Y. Leaders to Redistrict After Supreme Court Ruling
summary: The House minority leader, Hakeem Jeffries, sent a familiar emissary to the New York State Capitol to convey the party’s urgency to draw more favorable maps.
